summary: Phase 10.2 — Reports + Notifications tested end-to-end. Backend: 36/36 pytest cases passing in /app/backend/tests/test_reports.py covering all 10 /api/reports/* endpoints (admin-200, agent-403), JSON shape validation (Lead/Property/Client/Deal/Agent), date-range subset behavior on /leads & /deals, CSV exports (text/csv;charset=utf-8 + UTF-8 BOM + Content-Disposition attachment, parseable rows), and /api/notifications (shape, kind enum, sort order newest-first, RBAC scoping, JWT required). Regression: 8/8 prior module smoke tests still 200. Frontend: ReportsPage renders all 5 sections with stable section-level testids; CSV blob download works; reports-print-button correctly triggers window.print() (verified via stub); date filter Apply+Clear refetches and resets; ProtectedRoute correctly blocks agents from /reports (agent navigated and reports-page testid never mounts, only 403 console errors). Notifications panel: bell visible, badge shows 9 unread, panel opens with 9 items, summary text formatting correct ('9 recent · 9 unread'), Mark-read updates to '9 recent · 0 unread' and hides both badge and Mark-read button, clicking an item navigates to /deals/{id} and closes the dropdown.

issues: Recharts emits 'width(-1) and height(-1)' console warnings during initial mount because ChartContainer renders inside a hidden/collapsed section before measurement. Functional impact: none (charts render correctly once visible), but the warning noise should be silenced by setting a minHeight or rendering charts only after data + layout settle.

action_items: (Optional polish) Suppress Recharts 'width(-1)/height(-1)' warnings by guarding ChartContainer with a non-zero minHeight or by deferring chart render until after first paint.

(Optional) Consider exposing the 5 report-section stat-card testids during the skeleton/loading state too, so e2e tests don't depend on completion of 5 parallel network calls before they can assert presence.
critical_code_review_comments: report.service.ts uses date_trunc with createdAt to bucket revenueTrend — this means deals created in month X but WON in a later month land in month X's bucket. Acceptable for v1 because the schema has no 'wonAt' timestamp, but worth flagging.

notification.service.ts builds 3 independent Prisma queries and merges in memory — fine for the 5×3 cap but if PER_KIND_LIMIT grows this becomes a hot path; consider pushing the merge to SQL with UNION ALL later.
report.controller.ts parseRange() silently drops malformed date strings instead of returning 400. Low risk (Date('foo') is NaN-handled) but UX could be improved.
ReportsPage.tsx fetchAll uses Promise.all — a single failing endpoint nukes all 5 sections. Consider Promise.allSettled so partial failures still render the rest.

NotificationPanel.tsx unread counting depends solely on item[0].createdAt as the new lastRead marker; if items[0] has identical createdAt to other items, the strict-greater comparison may still mark them unread on next render. Edge case only.

success_rate.frontend: 100% on functional checks (CSV download, print, date filter, mark-read, agent block, item navigation)

test_credentials: Admin: admin@realestate.com / Admin@2036  |  Agent: agent@realestate.com / Agent@2036
seed_data_creation: None — relied on the pre-seeded Neon DB described in the request (9 leads, 3 properties all AVAILABLE, 3 clients (1 linked + 2 unlinked), 2 deals incl. 1 WON in current month, 7 agents).
context_for_next_testing_agent: test_reports.py is the authoritative regression for /api/reports/* and /api/notifications. Reports page lives at /reports (ADMIN). Mark-as-read is purely frontend via localStorage key notif:lastRead:{userId}. Print/PDF is window.print(); stub it before clicking [data-testid=reports-print-button]. The notifications dropdown is a Radix DropdownMenu portal — query via [data-testid=notifications-panel] which is on the menu content node. NOTE FOR PLAYWRIGHT: when validating per-section stat-card testids (e.g. report-leads-total) wait ~3–4s after navigating to /reports for the 5 parallel report queries to resolve; otherwise the section root is visible but its children are still in the Skeleton state.
rca of the issue: No bugs found. The only deviation from expectations during the Playwright run was the inner section testids (report-leads-total, report-deals-status-row-WON, report-agents-table, etc.) reporting visible=false on the first pass — this is a TEST-TIMING artifact, not a functional bug. The page conditionally renders <StatCard> children only once `loading=false && report!=null`, and at 2s after navigation not all 5 endpoints had resolved. Confirmed by the fact that the very next interactions on the same page (CSV download, print, date-range Apply/Clear) all succeeded, which require the same data path.
